description: Login page SaaS redesign + security hardening — timing-safe comparison, rate limiting, CSRF protection, and polished centered card UI

userStories.title: Timing-safe secret comparison in login API
userStories.description: As the site owner, I want login attempts compared using constant-time string comparison so an attacker cannot infer the secret length or value via response timing.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace plain `===` comparison in `app/api/login/route.ts` with `crypto.timingSafeEqual`
Use `crypto.createHmac('sha256', 'pm-field-log').update(value).digest()` on both the provided secret and the stored secret before comparing, so buffers are always the same length
Import `crypto` from Node.js built-ins — no new packages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rate limit failed login attempts via KV
userStories.description: As the site owner, I want failed login attempts rate-limited by IP so brute-force attacks are blocked automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: On each failed attempt, call `kv.incr('ratelimit:login:{ip}')` where ip is the first value from `x-forwarded-for` header (split on comma, trim)
After incrementing, call `kv.expire('ratelimit:login:{ip}', 900)` to set 15-minute TTL
Before comparing the secret, read the counter with `kv.get('ratelimit:login:{ip}')`; if value >= 5, return 429 JSON `{ error: 'Too many attempts. Try again in 15 minutes.' }`
On successful login, call `kv.del('ratelimit:login:{ip}')` to clear the counter
Rate limit check runs before secret comparison
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CSRF / Origin header validation on login POST
userStories.description: As the site owner, I want the login API to reject cross-site requests so forged POSTs from other origins cannot authenticate an attacker.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/api/login/route.ts`, read the `Origin` header from the incoming request
Derive the allowed origin from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` if set, otherwise fall back to the `Host` header prefixed with `https://`
Also allow origins ending in `.vercel.app` to support preview deployments
Also allow `http://localhost:3000` for local dev
If `Origin` header is missing or does not match any allowed origin, return 403 JSON `{ error: 'Forbidden' }`
Valid same-origin requests proceed to rate-limit check then secret comparison
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redesigned login page — centered SaaS card
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the login page to look like a polished SaaS product so the site feels credible and professional end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Page uses a full-viewport centered layout (`min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center`) with `bg-ink/5` background — distinct from the main content area
Card is max-width 400px, uses `bg-card border border-line rounded-xl shadow-md p-8`
Card header shows site name 'Growth in Practice' in `font-display font-bold text-xl` and a subtitle 'Owner access' in `font-mono text-xs text-terracotta uppercase tracking-widest`
Password input is full-width, uses `font-mono`, has `placeholder='Enter your secret'`, focus ring is `focus:border-forest`
Submit button is full-width, `bg-forest text-paper font-display font-semibold`, disabled + shows 'Signing in…' during loading
Wrong secret error renders as `text-terracotta text-sm` inline below the input
Rate limit (429) error renders as `text-terracotta text-sm` with the message 'Too many attempts. Try again in 15 minutes.'
Network/unknown error renders as `text-terracotta text-sm` with 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'
The sidebar and main layout are NOT rendered on this page (login page has its own full-page layout)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
